message:
feat(interpreter): add first-class Closure value type with scope chain save/restore

Introduces proper closure semantics to the baseline interpreter by
adding a dedicated `Value::Closure(u32)` variant and scope chain
lifecycle management:

- New `Value::Closure(u32)`: references an entry in the interpreter's
  closure store. Closures carry both a function_index and a captured
  scope chain snapshot. Reports typeof as "function" and is truthy,
  matching ECMAScript semantics.

- Scope chain save/restore on closure calls: when calling a closure,
  the entire caller scope chain is saved in the `CallFrame` (via new
  `saved_scope_chain: Option<Vec<ScopeFrame>>` field) before being
  replaced with the closure's captured environment. On return, the
  saved chain is restored. Plain (non-closure) function calls continue
  to use the lightweight pop-to-depth strategy.

- Closure-aware Call and Construct instructions: both now resolve the
  callee to (func_idx, Option<captured_env>) via a shared match on
  Function/Closure variants before dispatching. This eliminates the
  previous linear scan of `self.closures` by function_index in favor
  of direct indexing by closure_id.

This is the foundation for supporting higher-order functions, callbacks,
and module-scoped state — prerequisites for real-world JS workloads
beyond arithmetic and simple control flow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
